comment: 25yo male, long-term survivor of childhood B-cell ALL diagnosed age 5, completed curative-intent therapy at age 8 (induction + consolidation per legacy COG protocol: cumulative doxorubicin ~280 mg/m² + cyclophosphamide + cranial radiotherapy 18 Gy for CNS prophylaxis era-typical). Now 17 years post-treatment, no personal cancer recurrence, no current malignancy or heart failure. Multiple iatrogenic late-effects RFs expected to fire: (1) RF-IATROGENIC-ANTHRACYCLINE-LATE-CARDIO-PREVENTION via cumulative ≥250 mg/m² + ≥5y post-treatment + pediatric-exposure branches → COG-LTFU echocardiogram cadence q2-5y; (2) RF-IATROGENIC-PRIOR-RADIATION-PREVENTION via ≥5y + childhood-cancer-RT branches → second-primary surveillance for radiation-induced thyroid / breast / CNS / sarcoma malignancies. Validates v0.3 multi-RF stacking on pediatric-survivor cohort. Note: original task title mentions 'lung cancer survivor' but childhood ALL is the underlying cohort — title preserved.
